question: You write an @Around advice but forget to call joinPoint.proceed(). What happens?
options: Spring detects the missing call and throws an AspectException at startup
The intercepted method executes normally without the around logic
The intercepted method never runs — the around advice returns null (or whatever the advice returns)
Spring calls proceed() automatically after the around advice completes

question: What is the difference between @After and @AfterReturning?
options: @After runs after successful return only; @AfterReturning runs always, even on exception
@After always runs (success or exception); @AfterReturning runs only on successful return and can access the return value
@After is for void methods; @AfterReturning is for methods that return a value
They are identical — both run after the method, regardless of outcome

question: Which pointcut expression matches all methods in any class inside the com.example.service package?
options: within(com.example.service.*)
execution(* com.example.service.*.*(..))
@within(org.springframework.stereotype.Service)
call(* com.example.service.*.*(..))

question: Why would you define a @Pointcut method instead of repeating the expression string in every advice?
options: @Pointcut methods are evaluated at compile time, making them faster
Repeated strings are not valid — Spring requires a single @Pointcut definition
A named pointcut lets you change the expression in one place and all advices using it update automatically
@Pointcut is required for @Around but optional for @Before and @After

question: An @Aspect class is correctly written but none of its advices run. What is the most likely cause?
options: The aspect class is missing @Component — Spring never registered it as a bean
AOP only works with @Repository and @Controller beans, not @Service
The pointcut expression must start with 'com.example' to be valid
@EnableAspectJAutoProxy must be added to every @Service class that should be intercepted
